explanations.1: This line tells the spell-checker to ignore this file so it doesn't complain about Docker-specific terms or configuration values.
explanations.7: The comment explains that UID and GID are numeric identifiers for users and groups in Linux; you can find your own values by running those commands if your system is different.
explanations.10: The comment is clarifying that while this USERNAME variable is set, Docker doesn't actually care what name you give it for this setup to work.
explanations.16: This line creates a two-way bridge between your computer's current folder and the `/opt/behat` folder inside the container, so changes in one place automatically appear in the other.
